name: Monday.com API
what_it_does: Monday.com's GraphQL API for managing boards, items, columns, and workflows in Monday.com's work management and CRM platform.
use_cases: Creating and updating board items from agent workflows
Reading board data for automated reporting and dashboards
Syncing CRM and project data between Monday.com and external systems
Managing team workflows and approval processes via agents
not_for: Developers who prefer REST over GraphQL
best_when: An agent needs to interact with Monday.com boards and items as part of a workflow automation tied to an existing Monday.com deployment.
avoid_when: Your team doesn't use Monday.com, you prefer REST APIs, or you have tight budget constraints.

auth.notes: Personal API tokens for direct access. OAuth2 for building apps on the Monday marketplace. Tokens grant account-wide access. Simple bearer token for agents.

pricing.notes: Free tier is very limited (2 seats). Most real use cases require Standard or Pro. Enterprise features (SAML, advanced security) require Enterprise tier.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GraphQL errors returned in errors array with message field. Column value errors can be cryptic. Documentation has improved but GraphQL introspection is the most reliable reference.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency mechanism. Item creation via GraphQL mutation is not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: GraphQL-only API means agents must construct queries/mutations — no simple REST calls
Column values use a complex JSON string format that differs per column type
Board and item IDs are required for all operations — human-readable names require lookup
Complexity scoring limits per request — complex queries may be rejected
Webhook verification requires challenge-response during registration
Account-level vs board-level permissions can cause confusing 403 errors
